pages.title: About | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central
pages.meta_description: Rachael Maskell MP for York Central, Member of Parliament
pages.text_excerpt: About | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About News Contact Newsletter Cost Of Living Campaign How to help How to get support Food Banks York Food Drive York Food Drive – Food Donation Weekend More Search Search the website Search Menu About Rachael Maskell MP Rachael Maskell was elected as the Member of Parliament for York Central in 2015 First elected in 2015, Rachael campaigns tirelessly for her constituents in York Central. With a career in the NHS, working as a senior physiotherapist in ITU and acute medicine and as the Head of Health at Unite, Rachael continues to use her expertise in Westminster to progress healthcare and protect the NHS and is Vice Chair of the Health and Social Care Health Select Committee. With the challenges people experience in York, Rachael has recently advanced legislation to regulate Short Term Holiday Lets, and speaks regularly on issues from housing and development, the economy, health and social care and the environment and climate. As a trade unionist, she also makes the case for advancing workers’ rights, not least through her Bill to tackle Bullying at Work. Central to Rachael’s work, is listening to the issues that constituents raise and bringing their voice to Parliament, especially the inequality and injustices that so many have faced under the Tories. Not only does she work across agencies in York to enhance services and support businesses, but takes up the issues residents need addressing. Contact details House of Commons, Houses of Parliament, London, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Danny Myers on behalf of Rachael Maskell, both at House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Cost Of Living | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central
pages.meta_description: Rachael Maskell MP for York Central, Member of Parliament
pages.text_excerpt: Cost Of Living | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About News Contact Newsletter Cost Of Living Campaign How to help How to get support Food Banks York Food Drive York Food Drive – Food Donation Weekend More Search Search the website Search Menu Cost Of Living Cost of living crisis With food, fuel and energy costs skyrocketing, rents across York spiralling, whilst wages stagnate, I know that many people are really worried about how they will get through this winter. I have spoken to so many residents who are having to make drastic choices, and will be cold or hungry if they can’t access support. The cost-of-living crisis is impacting on everyone, residents and businesses. As we did through the Covid pandemic, we need to come together as a city to support one another this winter. York is known for showing extraordinary acts of kindness and generosity. From reaching out to neighbours, to our incredible voluntary sector organisations, there are many ways that we can all play a role to help. The oil and gas producers, food retailers and housing developers continue to make significant profits, which is completely unacceptable when people cannot heat their homes, feed their families or have somewhere decent to live. I will be raising these issues in Parliament, while ensuring that you have the support you need in York. This handbook sets out ways you can get advice and practical support through this winter and how you can donate essentials, time and money to care for those in need. As your representative, I am always here for you and will work to support you in every way I can. How to help How to get support Contact Keep York Warm Donation Event Follow on social media Contact details House of Commons, Houses of Parliament, London, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Danny Myers on behalf of Rachael Maskell, both at House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: How to help | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central
pages.meta_description: Rachael Maskell MP for York Central, Member of Parliament
pages.text_excerpt: How to help | Rachael Maskell MP - Rachael Maskell - Labour and Co-operative MP for York Central Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About News Contact Newsletter Cost Of Living Campaign How to help How to get support Food Banks York Food Drive York Food Drive – Food Donation Weekend More Search Search the website Search Menu How to help How to help For Those That Can Give Donations If you are able to support people financially, a local charity, Two Ridings is working with Advice York Partnership (over 70 local organisations) to make direct grants to local people. To donate, go here: www.tworidingscf.org.uk/donate-to-the-cost-of-living-crisis Food York’s food-banks and other food distribution points are looking for food and financial donations. To help, please visit: www.rachaelmaskell.com/food Hygiene Products The Hygiene Bank is collecting personal care and cleaning products, like laundry detergent, baby wipes and deodorants. These are donated to local food banks and schools to address poverty: www.thehygienebank.com Keep York Warm New clothing including socks, thermals and blankets are much needed. As well as good quality winter coats: Our Keep York Warm donation event will take place on Fri 18 Nov & Sat 19 Nov , please drop off donations at the Central Methodist Church, St Saviourgate between 10am-3pm. Time To Help The Community York Volunteers There are many opportunities to volunteer and help our community. Check out the York CVS website www.yorkcvs.org.uk/volunteers or call 01904 621133 to find out more. Check in on your neighbours We have many local community and residents groups throughout the city, as well as mobile apps that keep us connected. Start or refresh your local street group and make time to check on your neighbours. A cup of tea and chat can help break the ice, if someone is feeling lonely or needs help but is unsure of where to turn. I am also working with churches and charities to identify warm hubs where people can go and get a friendly welcome, food and company.
